articles.title: World Cup Attendance Sets Record
articles.summary: The USMNT remains competitive heading into its Round of 16 match against Belgium despite the suspension of striker Folarin Balogun, while France remains the tournament favorite led by Kylian Mbappé. Bloomberg News Senior Sports Reporter Randall Williams joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on the couch of Bloomberg This Weekend and explains that the expanded FIFA World Cup has shattered attendance records and delivered a significant tourism and economic boost to host cities across the US, with packed stadiums and fan zones driving spending. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Nigeria says two nationals killed in South Africa amid rise of anti-migrant attacks
articles.summary: Nigeria's government said one of its nationals was reportedly killed by police officers "using gruesome interrogation techniques".

articles.title: America250 Looks Beyond July 4 Celebrations
articles.summary: America250 Commissioner Reginald Browne joins Bloomberg This Weekend and says the congressionally created initiative met its goal of engaging Americans in the nation's 250th anniversary celebrations and views the commemoration as an ongoing effort that will continue beyond this year's events. Browne explains to hosts David Gura and Christina Ruffini how private sponsors helped offset a federal funding shortfall and describes the Trump administration's Freedom250 initiative as complementary rather than competitive, broadening participation in the anniversary. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: New York Thunderstorms Raise Flood Risk as Heat Dissipates in US
articles.summary: New York City will have a cooler, soggier Sunday now that the massive heat dome across the US East that taxed power grids and snarled transit has started to crack, but temperatures in Washington and points south will still remain sultry through Sunday.

articles.title: SK Hynix Seeks Access to AI Investors in $29 Billion US Listing
articles.summary: This week’s $29 billion US stock-market listing for SK Hynix Inc. may be the biggest-ever first-time share sale by a foreign company, but it isn’t just about raising cash. It’s also about competing in the hottest corner of the global stock market — memory chips used in AI computing.

articles.title: Parents Weigh New Trump Accounts for Newborns
articles.summary: Parents can now begin contributing to newly launched Trump Accounts, which provide a $1,000 government-funded investment for eligible children born between 2025 and 2028. Bloomberg's Sarah Foster joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend and explains that financial advisers still generally favor 529 plans for education savings and that questions remain about the new accounts' tax treatment, withdrawal rules and future investment options. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: By The Way: Interesting Headlines You May Have Missed July 4th Week
articles.summary: Pony beers, household chores robots, 'bare nails' trend, and a new screen-time helper: Lisa Mateo shares with David Gura and Christina Ruffini the headlines explaining these topics that you and they may have missed this week. Watch Bloomberg This Weekend, Saturday and Sundays 7am EST. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iran War Looms Over NATO Summit
articles.summary: President Trump heads to Turkey this week, where NATO leaders will hold a crucial meeting. Bloomberg News Middle Easter Reporter Dan Williams joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend and explains that the NATO summit will focus on defense spending and support for Ukraine, but will also be shaped by the aftermath of the US-Iran conflict and questions about Washington's long-term commitment to the alliance. (Source: Bloomberg)

Iran and Qatar resume maritime trade: Iranian state media
articles.summary: Iran and Qatar resume maritime trade: Iranian state media Maritime trade between Iran and Qatar has resumed after a roughly five-month suspension, Iran's commercial attache in Doha told state media on Sunday. An interim deal between Tehran and Washington signed last month announced the end of hostilities after a four-month conflict, and mandated a return to pre-war maritime traffic in the Gulf, although transit in and out of the Gulf remains contested. Abbas Abdolkhani said shipping between Iran's Dayyer port and Qatar's Al-Ruwais port had resumed following coordinatio

articles.title: Netanyahu vows to not rebuild Gaza until Hamas surrenders its weapons
articles.summary: Netanyahu vows to not rebuild Gaza until Hamas surrenders its weapons Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said there will be no reconstruction of Gaza until Hamas is fully disarmed.  Netanyahu made the remarks on Sunday after a cabinet meeting.

articles.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guard diverts six ships from Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard diverts six ships from Strait of Hormuz Six ships changed course from the Omani corridor in the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) patrolled the area. Maritime intelligence firm Windward reported the diversion and said that, since Saturday, two of the ships diverted from the route that runs closer to Oman than Iran have sailed towards Iran, while the four others have returned to the Gulf. The IRGC navy has been issuing radio warnings to ships using routes other than what Iran describes as its "safe route", it added.

articles.title: Cargo vessel attacked near Yemen's Hodeidah, UK maritime agency says
articles.summary: Cargo vessel attacked near Yemen's Hodeidah, UK maritime agency says A cargo vessel has come under attack by unidentified armed assailants off Yemen's Red Sea coast, according to the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO). The UKMTO said it received a distress call from the vessel while it was around 56km (30 nautical miles) southwest of the Yemeni port city of Hodeidah. The agency said authorities are investigating the incident and advised ships transiting the area to exercise caution and report any suspicious activity. No group has immediately claimed responsibility for the attack.

State TV showed Mostafa, Meysam and Masoud Khamenei praying behind the coffins laid out in the vast courtyard of Tehran's Imam Khomeini Grand Mosalla, a sprawling religious complex. Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala to receive Khamenei’s remains
articles.summary: Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala to receive Khamenei’s remains The week-long funeral for former Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei will extend beyond Iran, with ceremonies scheduled in the Iraqi cities of Najaf and Karbala later this week, according to Al Jazeera. After public mourning events in Tehran and a commemoration ceremony in Qom, Khamenei’s remains are expected to be flown to Iraq on Wednesday. Ceremonies will be held in Najaf and Karbala, home to the shrines of Imam Ali and Imam Hussein, before the body is returned to Iran for burial in Mashhad at the Imam Reza shrine.  Mourne

articles.summary: Tehran metro records seven million trips for Khamenei funeral Tehran’s metro system recorded more than 7 million passenger trips between Saturday evening and Sunday morning as large crowds travelled to attend funeral ceremonies for former Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei, according to figures cited by Tasnim News Agency. The funeral at Tehran’s Imam Khomeini Mosalla is continuing throughout the day, with organisers urging attendees to use public transport. articles.summary: Funeral prayers held for Khamenei’s infant granddaughter killed in US-Israeli strike Funeral prayers were also held for four members of Ali Khamenei’s family who were killed in the joint US-Israeli strike on 28 February, including his 14-month-old granddaughter, Zahra Mohammadi Golpayegani. Footage published by Tasnim news agency showed the infant’s coffin placed beside Khamenei’s during the ceremony. articles.title: Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran
articles.summary: Khamenei’s sons attend funeral prayers in Tehran The Associated Press reported that Ali Khamenei’s sons Masoud, Meysam and Mostafa attended funeral prayers for the late Iranian supreme leader in Tehran on Sunday. The ceremony was also attended by Ahmad Vahidi, commander of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. articles.title: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join
articles.summary: Funeral ceremonies continue for Khamenei, senior officials join Funeral prayers were held for Iran’s late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei on Sunday at Tehran’s Grand Mosalla complex, marking the second day of ceremonies that have drawn large crowds to the capital. President Masoud Pezeshkian, parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf and other senior officials attended the prayers, which were led by prominent Shia cleric Jafar Sobhani. Khamenei’s coffin, draped in the Iranian flag, was placed alongside the coffins of four relatives who were also killed in the 28 February strikes by US and Israel

IRNA reported that Brigadier General Ahmad Vahidi, Commander-in-Chief of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), who made his first public appearance on Friday after months of absence from the public eye, was among those who offered the prayers.
